items.title: Professional Development in Technology
items.description: Introduction to career and leadership opportunities, employer expectations, job search skills, and placement resources within technology fields of study.

items.title: Intro Technical Drawing & Constraint-Based Solid Modeling
items.description: Introduction to technical drawing with emphasis on CAD constraint-based solid modeling, sketching, and basic blueprint reading.

items.title: Introduction To Manufacturing Processes
items.description: Secondary material processes including industrial machinery usage and study in forming, casting, separating, joining, and conditioning.

items.title: Fundamentals of Power Technology
items.description: Principles of electrical, electronic, mechanical, and fluid power components and systems as they apply to industrial applications.

items.title: Graphic Communications Technology
items.description: Introduction to the history, vocabulary, tools, materials, and process of the graphic communications industry.

items.title: Introduction To Computer Systems Technology
items.description: Study and use of basic methods and software used in computer applications for imaging, graphics, and communications.

items.title: Constraint-Based Solid Modeling And Production Drawings
items.description: Intermediate course focusing on constraint-based solid modeling, technical drawing practices, and blueprint/tolerance interpretation.

items.title: Graphic Media Composition
items.description: Study of graphic media production technology to plan and create documents including advertisements, brochures, books, packages, and e-books.

items.title: Image Capture And Editing
items.description: Digital photography, scanning, image editing software tools, PostScript output, halftones, tone reproduction, densitometry, and reproduction concerns.

items.title: Digital Media Applications
items.description: Study of technology used for digital media and communications.

items.title: Print Media Production
items.description: Study of printing production processes including lithography, flexography, screen printing, and digital printing.

items.title: Managing Technological Systems
items.description: Principles, practices and methods used to plan, organize, lead, and control technological systems.

items.title: Computer Generated Imagery
items.description: Foundational skills in modeling, texturing, rigging, lighting, animating, and rendering using computer systems.

items.title: Engineering Economics
items.description: Applied economic techniques used to determine the relative worth of alternative systems, products, and services.

items.title: Computer Numerical Control (CNC) & Machining
items.description: Introduction to metals machining processes with emphasis on automation using CNC.

items.title: Robotic Systems Integration
items.description: Introduction to robotics including common applications, programming, safety, and integration principles.

items.title: Electric Circuits & Machines
items.description: Electrical principles and applications including circuit diagrams, switches, relays, motors, and transformers.

items.title: Automated Fluid Power Systems
items.description: Theory and operation of fluid power circuits and intermediate level PLC programming.

items.title: Industrial Plastics
items.description: Resins, processing, fabrication, injection molding, extrusion, rotational molding, compression molding, thermoforming, fiberglass lay-up, identification and testing.

items.title: Mechanical Properties of Materials
items.description: Theory and practice of standardized testing procedures to determine the modulus and strength of engineering materials.

items.title: Project Management
items.description: Fundamentals of project management emphasizing planning techniques to meet stakeholder expectations regarding scope, time, cost, and quality.

items.title: Prepress Technology
items.description: Utilization of advanced pre-press methods and technologies in the creation and management of print media.

items.title: Manufacturing Engineering Intern
items.description: Caterpillar Inc.
bullets: Analyzed Key Performance Indicators from machine data to discover the source of downtime
Built spreadsheets and tools in Microsoft Excel to understand part workflow and bottlenecks
Collaborated between machine operators and management to improve relationships and communication

items.title: Quality Systems For Technology
items.description: A managed quality system using statistical tools; control charts, paretos, histograms, scattergrams, flowcharts and problem-solving techniques.

items.title: Package Design And Production
items.description: Study of design, materials, and production processes used in the manufacture of packages and displays.

items.title: Cross-Media Technology
items.description: Technology and processes used in cross-media production, with emphasis on interactive digital print products.

items.title: Print Production Planning and Profitability
items.description: Printing production workflow analysis, cost estimating, scheduling, job costing, and profitability.

items.title: Color Management
items.description: Study of color theory and measurement, device calibration and profiling, color reproduction variables, color proofing, and color management workflow.

items.title: Graphic Communication Business Practices
items.description: Current management trends with emphasis on production management and legal issues for graphic communications businesses.

items.title: E-Publishing Management
items.description: Study of ePublishing technology, media production management, and digital asset management for graphic communications businesses.

items.title: Packaging & Creative Intern
items.description: Quad Graphics
bullets: Walked through production facilities in Franklin & Sussex
Designed and updated graphics for consumer packaging
Prepared packaging artwork using Esko tools and Adobe Illustrator
Updated marketing materials to align with new company rebrand
Created a new demo website for Packaging Insight (South Carolina)

items.title: Manufacturing Organization And Management
items.description: The study of industrial production systems including product, manufacturing, and plant engineering through managing a production project.

items.title: Print Production Assistant
items.description: The Copy Shop
bullets: Communicated with customers about job specifications including sizing, substrate choice, bleeds, crops, and file conversions
Prepared impositions to maximize ink efficiency and minimize material waste
Calibrated printers for paper weights, sizes, and coatings
Printed, bound, resized, and priced blueprints
Organized physical assets and filed customer orders
Brought in treats to boost team morale

items.title: Application Engineer
items.description: Echo Electric
bullets: Designed and managed build prints and electrical schematics for industrial applications
Operated machinery and equipment safely and effectively
Programmed industrial control panels and HMIs with Phoenix Contact's PLCNext Engineer
Created processes and procedures for future engineers to follow
Communicated with customers about project details, timelines, part delivery
Resolved build issues with management and technicians
Improved and updated digital file organization and structure

items.title: Delivery Driver
items.description: Doordash
bullets: Deliver food and groceries to customers in a friendly and timely manner.
Learning alternative routes to deliver orders faster.
Find customer drop off locations and follow customer instructions.
